'''Paul Sykes''' (born 11 August 1981) is an English professional rugby league footballer who plays as a {{rlp|so}} for the [[Dewsbury Rams]] in the [[RFL Championship]].

Sykes came through the ranks at [[Bradford Bulls]] making his debut in 1999. He left the Bulls in 2002 for [[London Broncos]] before resigning for them in 2008. Sykes also spent time at [[Wakefield Trinity]] and [[Featherstone Rovers]].

Sykes made his debut for [[England national rugby league team|England national team]] in 2005 and also went on to represent [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]].

In 2022, he was appointed interim head-coach at [[Dewsbury Rams]] following the departure of [[Lee Greenwood]].<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

At age 44, he is the oldest English professional rugby league player playing for [[Dewsbury Rams]] in [[RFL League 1|League 1]].
In 2025, Reilly made rugby league world history, playing an outstanding 27 seasons.

On 28 March 2026 he was inducted into the RFL Roll of Honour<ref>{{cite web|website=www.rugby-league.com|date=28 Mar 2026|title=Four new names added to RFL's Roll of Honour|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/64664/four-new-names-added-to-rfls-roll-of-honour}}</ref>

==Background==
Sykes was born in [[Dewsbury]], [[West Yorkshire]], England.

==Bradford==
Paul Sykes is a product of [[Bradford Bulls]] academy, making his breakthrough in the 1999 Bradford season during 1999's [[Super League IV]]. However opportunities at [[Odsal Stadium]] were limited and so Sykes went on loan spells at London in both the 2001 and 2002 seasons.

In his first spell at Bradford Bulls, he was loaned out to [[Wakefield RFC]] for which he played one game of Rugby Union.
[[File:Paul Sykes.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Sykes playing for Harlequins Rugby League]]

==Move to London==
With the promise of more regular first XIII rugby Sykes moved south to join the [[London Broncos#1994–2005: Broncos and Super League|London Broncos]] in a permanent deal in August 2002.

Sykes soon settled back to life in the capital and representative honours were gained with an appearance for England "A" against New Zealand in October, followed by a place on the successful "A" team tour to Fiji and Tonga.

Sykes has further earned England caps against Russia and Wales in 2003 and France and New Zealand in 2005.

Sykes originally came to the club as full-back, but the arrival of club captain [[Mark McLinden]] has seen Sykes operate largely in the centres in Super League X and XI.

Sykes successfully converted during 2005 to the centre position where his powerful running and strong tackling are seen to their best advantage.

Super League XI saw Sykes ruled out for much of the season after damaging his kidneys in February. However he made a comeback towards the end of the season after initially being ruled out for the entire campaign

2005 was a record breaking year for Sykes. On 27 February against the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]], Sykes kicked 12 goals from 12 attempts to break a 17-year-old London club record.

Further records for most goals and most points followed at the season's end and in recognition of these achievements Sykes was voted Supporters' Player-of-the-Year for 2005.
Harlequins centre Sykes has confirmed his expected move to Bradford for next season on a two-year contract.

Sykes, who made his Great Britain début earlier this season, left Bradford on loan in 2002 to find first-team rugby.

Quins coach [[Brian McDermott (rugby league)|Brian McDermott]] also paid tribute to Sykes' contribution at [[the Stoop]].

==Bradford and the Wakefield Trinity Wildcats==
Sykes signed for Bradford Bulls in September 2007 as a replacement for the departing [[Ben Harris (rugby league)|Ben Harris]].

On rejoining Bradford he said: "A lot's changed at Bradford since the first time around and I think there's only [[Paul Deacon]] and [[Jamie Langley]] still there. "But I'm ready for a fresh challenge and I'm hoping to move back north to win some silverware. Bradford are always challenging and I'm glad I'm going to be a part of that. "Also, my girlfriend has just given birth to our first child as well and we want to be closer to our family." Boss [[Steve McNamara]] reckons he has picked up a rare talent, and added "I'm absolutely delighted to have secured an outstanding English centre and thank the board for making the deal possible".

In the 2012 season Sykes was loaned out to fellow West Yorkshire club the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] after only playing 2 games for Bradford Bulls. Here he managed to help lead the Wakefield Trinity Wildcats into the play-offs ironically at the expense of Bradford Bulls. Sykes soon signed a permanent one-year deal with the Wakefield Trinity Wildcats for the 2013 season.

==Great Britain==
In June 2007 Sykes was called up to the [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] squad<ref>{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/6744909.stm|title=Eight new faces in Lions squad|publisher=BBC|date=12 June 2007|access-date=12 June 2007}}</ref><ref name="Melling and Sykes named in GB squad">{{cite web|url=http://www.quins.co.uk/newspage.ink?nid=37438&newstype=n&matchid=&storytype=RL&Refid=|title=Melling and Sykes named in GB squad|publisher=Quins RL|date=13 June 2007|access-date=13 June 2007|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070930224114/http://www.quins.co.uk/newspage.ink?nid=37438&newstype=n&matchid=&storytype=RL&Refid=|archive-date=30 September 2007|df=dmy-all}}</ref> and will play in the [[Rugby league positions#Centre|centres]] in the [[Test match (rugby league)|Test match]] against [[France national rugby league team|France]].<ref name=" New-look GB select five new caps">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/6765035.stm|title= New-look GB select five new caps|publisher=BBC|date=20 June 2007|access-date=20 June 2007}}</ref>

Sykes is joined in the [[Great Britain national rugby league team#Current squad|Great Britain]] Test side by [[London Broncos#2006–2011: Harlequins Rugby League|Harlequins RL]] teammate [[Chris Melling (rugby league)|Chris Melling]].<ref name="Melling and Sykes to play at Headingley">{{cite web|url=http://www.quins.co.uk/newspage.ink?nid=37475&newstype=n&matchid=&storytype=RL&Refid=|title=Melling and Sykes to play at Headingley|publisher=Quins|date=20 June 2007|access-date=20 June 2007|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070930224312/http://www.quins.co.uk/newspage.ink?nid=37475&newstype=n&matchid=&storytype=RL&Refid=|archive-date=30 September 2007|df=dmy-all}}</ref>

Sykes made his Great Britain début in the 42–14 victory over the [[France national rugby league team|French]] on 22 June 2007, scoring one try.<ref name="Great Britain 42-14 France">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/6228054.stm|title= Great Britain 42–14 France|publisher=BBC|date=22 June 2007|access-date=24 June 2007}}</ref><ref name="Hungry Lions Please Smith">{{cite web|url=http://www.sportinglife.com/rugbyleague/news/story_get.cgi?STORY_NAME=rleague/07/06/22/RUGBYL_Great_Britain_Quotes.html|title=Hungry Lions Please Smith|publisher=SportingLife|date=22 June 2007|access-date=24 June 2007|archive-date=30 September 2007|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070930050258/http://www.sportinglife.com/rugbyleague/news/story_get.cgi?STORY_NAME=rleague%2F07%2F06%2F22%2FRUGBYL_Great_Britain_Quotes.html|url-status=dead}}</ref>

==England==
In September 2008 he was named in the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup squads#England|England training squad]] for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]],<ref name="Myler gets England call">{{cite web|url=http://www.englandrl.co.uk/article.php?id=11890|title=Myler gets England call|publisher=England Rugby League|date=29 September 2008|access-date=3 October 2008|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20081007232803/http://www.englandrl.co.uk/article.php?id=11890|archive-date=7 October 2008|df=dmy-all}}</ref> and in October 2008 he was named in the final 24-man [[2008 Rugby League World Cup squads#England|England]] squad.<ref name="Purdham earns World Cup call-up">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/7656553.stm|title=Purdham earns World Cup call-up|publisher=BBC|date=7 October 2008|access-date=7 October 2008}}</ref>

He has been named in the [[England national rugby league team|England]] team to face [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] at the [[Keepmoat Stadium]], [[Doncaster]], prior to England's departure for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="Gleeson to lead new-look England">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/7642490.stm|title=Gleeson to lead new-look England|publisher=BBC|date=9 October 2008|access-date=10 October 2008}}</ref>

Sykes has also represented the [[England national rugby league team|England]] in their warm up match against [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] before the 2009 Four Nations.
